title: Web channel: generic context + read-aloud + doc render

tests.summary: 14 passed (generic context contract, backward-compatible render, doc-render garrison://+http link handling, XSS sanitization)

e2e.summary: shell renders the generic chat surface (Operative title, composer, mode chips); the READ-ALOUD control is present (FINDING 14); the ?mode=james&context= URL is accepted without breaking the shell (generic — no kanban knowledge); responsive at 390px; no console errors. Operative test interface (src/app/run/page.tsx + spike) git-confirmed UNCHANGED.

codexReview.notes: fixed a markdown-link XSS (scheme allowlist + attr escape) + a context-opacity violation

codexPwTest.summary: codex sandbox cannot launch Chromium (same MachPort limitation as kanban-board-ui). Same-runtime Playwright render done; cross-model REVIEW (3A approve r2) is the second-model opinion.

by: vision review of the rendered shell
designAudit.notes: generic dark chat surface; read-aloud + mic controls; markdown composer; no kanban/dev-env labels (stays generic). No emoji in UI.

video.notes: render walkthrough of the generic surface + read-aloud (desktop + phone). The generic-context/doc-render/XSS contract is the committed unit suite (14).

blockers.cause: a live James-mode reply rendering a produced document requires the full gateway+operative stack (spawns a real claude TUI on the Max account) — heavy + not stood up in this autonomous headless run
blockers.remediationAttempted: rendered the generic surface + read-aloud control via Playwright; verified the generic-context + doc-render + XSS contract via the committed unit suite (14 tests); confirmed the operative test interface untouched. The live conversation is a documented deferral, not a defect.
